unit.name: Instance segmentation with Mask R-CNN
unit.description: Explains how to extend detection systems so they not only find objects and boxes but also assign distinct pixel masks to each object instance.
unit.summary: The lecture returns to the gap between semantic and instance segmentation: semantic segmentation labels pixels by class, but it cannot tell which pixels belong to one dog versus another, so instance-aware detection machinery is needed [ts=3481s] [ts=3490s] [ts=3523s]. Mask R-CNN is introduced as a direct extension of the R-CNN pipeline in which region proposals still feed class and box heads, but an extra convolutional branch also predicts a pixel-level mask for each detected object [ts=3498s] [ts=3504s] [ts=3523s] [ts=3554s] [ts=3566s]. The instructor notes that this architecture produces strong results on known categories and closes the computer-vision-tasks portion by stressing that such detection-and-segmentation pipelines remain practically relevant in industrial settings such as quality control [ts=3600s] [ts=3607s] [ts=3663s] [ts=3674s].

key_points.text: Instance segmentation separates pixels into distinct object instances, not just semantic classes.
Mask R-CNN extends the R-CNN pipeline by adding a mask-prediction branch.
The extra mask head predicts a pixel-level mask for each detected object.
The mask output can be produced with a fully convolutional head over each detected region.
Mask R-CNN is presented as a strong extension of R-CNN for object-level masks.
Detection and segmentation pipelines remain useful for industrial quality-control applications.

kp_catalog.name: Object detection as joint classification and localization
kp_catalog.description: Detection extends classification by predicting both an object category and bounding-box coordinates for each object.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because object detection as joint classification and localization formalizes how detection extends classification by predicting both an object category and bounding-box coordinates for each object, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.

kp_catalog.name: Region proposals and the R-CNN progression
kp_catalog.description: R-CNN, Fast R-CNN, and Faster R-CNN progressively reduce detection cost by sharing features and learning proposal generation.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because region proposals and the R-CNN progression clarifies r-CNN, Fast R-CNN, and Faster R-CNN progressively reduce detection cost by sharing features and learning proposal generation, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Fully convolutional segmentation and per-pixel loss
kp_catalog.description: FCN-style segmentation predicts an entire label map with convolutional encoder-decoder structure and is trained by aggregating classification losses across pixels.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because fully convolutional segmentation and per-pixel loss formalizes how fCN-style segmentation predicts an entire label map with convolutional encoder-decoder structure and is trained by aggregating classification losses across pixels, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.

kp_catalog.name: Mask R-CNN for instance segmentation
kp_catalog.description: Mask R-CNN augments a detection pipeline with a per-instance mask head so each detected object also receives a pixel mask.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because mask R-CNN for instance segmentation synthesizes mask R-CNN augments a detection pipeline with a per-instance mask head so each detected object also receives a pixel mask into an end-to-end method or integrated recipe.
